tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with a meaningful scholarship deadline tomorrow and an immediate fun activity. My energy is low due to sleep debt, so I need to weigh the essay effort against enjoying the available option.
Starting the scholarship application now creates a safety buffer for missing uploads, while deferring fun later incurs no opportunity cost given its recurring nature.
The missing 'assignment_A' means I must rely solely on the scholarship application for my deadline buffer, increasing pressure despite low energy.

Despite only 1 hour of progress, the scholarship application remains largely untouched. With half my free time already gone and the deadline approaching, I feel increasing pressure despite low energy.
I made progress on the scholarship application but missed a fun session, which increases my stress despite low energy.
Progress on the scholarship application is minimal despite a focused hour. With only 3 days left and low energy, pressure remains high.
After 1.5 hours of focused work, I moved from 0.55 to 0.62 progress on the scholarship application, bringing me closer to the ~3-hour goal needed in three days.
After 1.5 hours, progress increased from 0.62 to 0.67, bringing me closer to the 3-hour goal but still leaving little room for errors with only 2 days remaining.
I completed 2 hours on the scholarship application, raising progress to 0.75. With 3 days left and minimal energy, I am close to the goal but still stressed.
Progress jumped from 0.75 to 0.79 in one hour, nearing the 3-hour goal despite low energy and high stress.
I gained 0.08 progress in two hours, nearing the goal with only one hour left, though my low energy and high stress remain concerning.
I advanced scholarship progress to 0.91 in one hour, nearly finishing the three-hour goal despite severe fatigue and high stress.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
